decision.selected_candidate_label: gray-box tri-evidence audit scorer bridging current CDI paired contract and aligned PIA/TMIA-DM packet surfaces
selection_reason: It is genuinely distinct from the admitted white-box loss-score family and from the closed shadow-LR follow-up.
It reuses already-landed gray-box evidence surfaces instead of reopening same-family I-B rescue, translated I-C churn, or latent-diffusion pseudo-comparability.
The missing execution gap is CPU-first and concrete: freeze TMIA-DM sample-identity export plus an offline tri-score canary before any GPU question is reopened.

candidates.label: I-C same-pair white-gray bridge replay on frozen 965/1278 pair
candidates.distinct_family_claim: Cross-permission bridge support is a white-gray causal replay question, not a white-box loss-score scorer follow-up.
candidates.bounded_hypothesis: If the frozen targeted mask can be replayed on the same packet under a same-spec gray-box contract, then targeted replay should beat matched random on both locality anchors and gray-box split-level low-FPR metrics.
candidates.cpu_first_prereq_cost: medium (~6-12h): add a same-pair gray-box evaluator that emits AUC/ASR/TPR@1%FPR/TPR@0.1%FPR on a fixed bridge packet while preserving alias and packet identity.
candidates.gpu_fire_cost: blocked until the same-spec gray-box release board exists; current candidate is not honest to fire.
candidates.four_metric_reporting_feasibility: not yet honest; current bridge artifacts still stop at locality anchors and member_control_score_gap.
kill_conditions: same_spec_reuse stays false after the CPU-first contract review
the same frozen packet still cannot emit a split-level four-metric board
the branch remains translated-contract-only on the frozen 965/1278 pair

candidates.label: I-B selected-channel delta follow-up on existing Finding NeMo bounded packet
candidates.distinct_family_claim: Finding NeMo locality review is still distinct from white-box loss-score scoring, but it remains inside the already-falsified localization-defense family.
candidates.bounded_hypothesis: Existing fixed-mask artifacts might expose one new selected_channel_abs_delta-style sample ranking that changes the quality-vs-defense reading without retraining or another extraction run.
candidates.cpu_first_prereq_cost: low (~2-4h): offline review over existing intervention-review outputs and locality-anchor artifacts.
candidates.gpu_fire_cost: none for scoping; any later GPU rerun would still be same-family rescue.
candidates.four_metric_reporting_feasibility: yes on the existing intervention-review surface, but only by re-reading an already closed falsifier packet.
kill_conditions: the offline audit only rephrases the existing falsifier rather than creating a new project-level question
no new bounded admitted-surface hypothesis appears above the current actual bounded falsifier

candidates.label: black-box second-signal scouting via step-wise dispersion / curvature-style score
candidates.distinct_family_claim: A black-box dispersion or curvature score would be a new signal family relative to recon, CLiD, semantic-aux, and the closed white-box loss-score line.
candidates.bounded_hypothesis: A multi-step dispersion score on existing recon-compatible artifacts might separate members without intermediate access strongly enough to justify a new family canary.
candidates.cpu_first_prereq_cost: high (~8-12h): freeze a runnable score contract, artifact schema, and asset reuse path on current black-box surfaces.
candidates.gpu_fire_cost: unclear and probably nontrivial; not honest to estimate below one new runner contract review.
candidates.four_metric_reporting_feasibility: possible only after a new scorer surface exists; not currently landed in the repo.
kill_conditions: the candidate cannot reuse existing recon or local public artifacts without new runner code
the first CPU canary still depends on a brand-new black-box interface rather than a frozen current surface

candidates.label: gray-box tri-evidence audit scorer over current CDI paired contract plus aligned PIA/TMIA-DM packet surfaces
candidates.distinct_family_claim: This is a collection-level gray-box evidence-aggregation question, not another PIA/TMIA switching threshold scan and not a white-box loss-score scorer family.
candidates.bounded_hypothesis: If the current bounded-positive PIA/TMIA-DM same-split actionability gain is real enough to matter beyond switching, then a frozen internal tri-score canary that combines current CDI paired-contract semantics with aligned TMIA-DM packet scores should beat at least one of the existing bounded baselines on low-FPR metrics without any new GPU run.
candidates.cpu_first_prereq_cost: medium (~4-8h): add a TMIA-DM sample-identity-preserving export or adapter on the existing aligned packet and freeze one offline tri-score evaluator under the current internal CDI contract.
candidates.gpu_fire_cost: none for the first canary; only after a positive CPU-first canary would a larger shared-surface rerun deserve separate review.
candidates.four_metric_reporting_feasibility: yes; the first canary can emit AUC/ASR/TPR@1%FPR/TPR@0.1%FPR on the aligned packet while keeping CDI-style internal contract fields and component reporting.
kill_conditions: TMIA-DM sample identity cannot be frozen from the current aligned packet artifacts without reopening GPU first
the tri-score canary fails to beat both current z-score-sum and current paired control-z-linear baselines on either AUC or low-FPR targets
the candidate drifts into another threshold-scan or same-family switching restatement

chosen_candidate.promotion_target: distinct bounded gray-box successor candidate for G1-A, still below headline and external evidence

cpu_first_prereq.name: tmiadm_identity_export_plus_triscore_offline_canary
deliverables: identity-preserving TMIA-DM sample export or deterministic adapter for the frozen aligned packet
one offline tri-score evaluator that emits four metrics plus current internal CDI contract fields
one canonical canary summary under Research/workspaces/gray-box/runs/

conditions: identity alignment cannot be frozen on current packet artifacts
the first offline tri-score canary does not beat current bounded baselines on at least one low-FPR target without collapsing AUC
the extension would require a new GPU run merely to establish packet identity

consumer_handoff.reason: Current higher-layer consumer contract can stay unchanged at scoping time because this candidate remains internal-only and preserves the existing contract-first gating pattern; no new Platform-facing field is being promoted in this dispatch.
